name: buddhist_philosophy
assumptions: All phenomena are impermanent (anicca) and without fixed essence (anatta)
Suffering (dukkha) arises from craving and ignorance; liberation is achievable
Dependent origination (pratityasamutpada) explains the causal structure of reality
Direct meditative insight complements rational analysis as a means of knowledge

norms.message: Should address or acknowledge the no-self doctrine where relevant
Should engage with emptiness (sunyata) when discussing ultimate reality
Should consider karmic causality and moral responsibility
Should engage with Buddhist epistemology (pramana theory)

criteria: acknowledgement of diversity of Buddhist schools
balance of analytic and contemplative approaches

bridge_warnings.explanation: Buddhist no-self (anatta) fundamentally denies what Cartesian philosophy of mind posits. No bridge concept exists that preserves both frameworks.
Nagarjuna's emptiness is not ontological absence but the deconstruction of inherent existence. Cannot be equated with Western nothingness or non-being.
